claim: GPT-2 (124M) can be reproduced in under one hour on cloud GPU at approximately $10 cost
actionable: Budget $10 and allocate 1 hour for GPU training when reproducing GPT-2 from scratch

claim: Weight tying between token embedding and output classification layer improves efficiency and can save ~30% of parameters in GPT-2
actionable: Share weights between input embedding and language model head (output projection) to reduce parameter count by ~40M parameters

claim: Residual layer outputs should be initialized with reduced variance (scaled by 1/sqrt(n) where n is number of layers) to prevent activation magnitude growth
actionable: Implement initialization scaling of residual layer outputs by 1/sqrt(2*num_layers) to keep residual stream variance stable

claim: TensorFloat32 enables 8x speedup with minimal accuracy impact by using 19-bit mantissa instead of 32-bit during GPU operations
actionable: Enable TF32 in PyTorch with torch.backends.cudnn.allow_tf32 = True for A100 GPUs

claim: Flash Attention achieves 7.6x speedup compared to standard attention by avoiding materialization of the attention matrix and reducing HBM bandwidth usage
actionable: Replace manual attention implementation with torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention which calls Flash Attention when available

claim: Rounding vocabulary size from 50257 to 50304 (next power-of-two aligned) improves training speed by 4% due to more efficient GPU kernel blocking
actionable: Pad vocabulary size to nearest power-of-two or highly divisible number for better GPU kernel efficiency

claim: GPT-3 uses cosine decay learning rate schedule with linear warmup over 375M tokens, decaying to 10% over 260B tokens for stable training
actionable: Implement cosine annealing learning rate schedule with ~1% of total steps for warmup and final learning rate at 10% of peak

claim: Global gradient norm clipping at 1.0 prevents training instability from large gradient spikes due to bad batches
actionable: Use torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0) after backward() to stabilize training

claim: Weight decay (0.1) should only apply to 2D matrices and embeddings, not biases or layer norm parameters which should have zero decay
actionable: Configure optimizer parameter groups: apply weight_decay=0.1 only to linear layer weights and embeddings, set 0 for biases and layer norms

claim: Autodetect available device (CUDA, MPS, CPU) to make code portable across hardware with no manual GPU specification needed
actionable: Use device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'mps' if torch.backends.mps.is_available() else 'cpu' at startup

claim: Pre-normalization (applying LayerNorm before attention/MLP) provides cleaner residual streams for gradient flow compared to post-normalization
actionable: Apply LayerNorm before each attention and MLP block rather than after to maintain uniform gradient distribution through residual pathway

claim: GPT-2 learns sinusoidal-like positional embeddings from scratch (not fixed), and these patterns emerge naturally during training
actionable: Initialize positional embeddings as learnable parameters and let them be optimized, rather than using fixed sinusoidal encodings

claim: Sample training data without replacement until epoch boundary is reached to ensure diverse gradient updates
actionable: Implement data loader that exhausts the corpus before repeating to ensure each example appears once per epoch

claim: Token embeddings that are semantically similar should have similar representations both at input and output, justifying weight tying
actionable: When designing architectures, consider weight tying between input/output embeddings when token similarity should be preserved consistently

claim: Attention is a communication/reduce operation where tokens exchange information, while MLP is a per-token mapping operation
actionable: Understand transformer blocks as alternating reduce (attention) and map (MLP) operations for information flow design

claim: GPU memory bandwidth (~2TB/s) is the primary bottleneck for most training workloads, not compute, requiring optimization of data movement
actionable: Profile GPU utilization; if below 60% utilization, prioritize reducing memory reads/writes (kernel fusion) over increasing FLOPs

claim: Using fused AdamW optimizer implementation reduces kernel launch overhead by combining all parameter updates into single kernel
actionable: Pass fused=True to torch.optim.AdamW when available to reduce optimizer step overhead on CUDA

claim: At initialization, loss should be ~ln(vocab_size) for uniform probability distribution, allowing sanity check on training setup
actionable: Verify initial loss equals ln(50257) ≈ 10.82 to confirm proper model initialization before training begins

claim: Use batch sizes that are powers of 2 (16, 32, 64, 128) for optimal GPU memory alignment and kernel efficiency
actionable: Set batch_size to nearest power of 2 to maximize GPU occupancy; avoid prime numbers or non-aligned sizes

claim: GPT-2 tokenizer achieves approximately 3:1 compression ratio (1000 characters ≈ 300 tokens)
actionable: When estimating token counts, multiply text length in bytes by 0.25 as approximation for GPT-2 tokenizer
